assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated IFRS financial statements for foreign currency risk arising from an intragroup dividend receivable/payable.
The dividend has been recognized as an intragroup receivable by one group entity and a corresponding payable by another group entity.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidation.
